dependencies.description: Requires Ethernet connectivity between aggregation partners
dependencies.name: Layer 2 connectivity

dependencies.description: Ports must be in a channel-group with mode active or passive
dependencies.name: Channel-group with LACP mode

dependencies.description: All member ports must have identical speed, duplex, VLAN, MTU configuration
dependencies.name: Matching member port parameters

dependencies.description: The core LACP daemon in SONiC. teamd manages all PortChannel (Linux team device) operations including LACPDU generation, reception, state machine execution, and aggregator selection. All PortChannels on the switch are managed by a single teamd container. If teamd dies, all PortChannels go down.
dependencies.name: teamd (teamd container)

dependencies.description: Translates PortChannel configuration from CONFIG_DB into teamd JSON configuration files. teammgrd watches CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL and PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER tables and writes /etc/teamd/<PortChannelName>.conf files that teamd reads for each PortChannel instance.
dependencies.name: teammgrd (swss container)

dependencies.description: Listens to teamd netlink events (port up/down, member joins/leaves) and synchronizes the resulting LAG state into APPL_DB LAG_TABLE and LAG_MEMBER_TABLE. orchagent then reads these tables to program the ASIC.
dependencies.name: teamsyncd (swss container)

dependencies.description: Consumes APPL_DB LAG_TABLE and LAG_MEMBER_TABLE entries from teamsyncd and translates them into SAI API calls to create and modify LAG objects and LAG member objects in ASIC_DB. Controls which ports are active in the ASIC forwarding LAG.
dependencies.name: orchagent (swss container)

dependencies.description: Monitors physical port link state changes via netlink and updates APPL_DB PORT_TABLE. teamd depends on accurate port link state information to determine which member ports are eligible for LACP negotiation.
dependencies.name: portsyncd (swss container)

dependencies.description: Source of truth for PortChannel configuration: PORTCHANNEL table (PortChannel name, min_links, fast_rate, mtu, admin_status) and PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER table (member port assignments). teammgrd reads from here to configure teamd.
dependencies.name: CONFIG_DB (Redis DB 4)

dependencies.description: LAG state publication bus between teamsyncd and orchagent. Contains LAG_TABLE (PortChannel operational state) and LAG_MEMBER_TABLE (per-member active status). orchagent reads these to drive ASIC programming.
dependencies.name: APPL_DB (Redis DB 0)

dependencies.description: Stores operational LACP and LAG state for monitoring and cross-daemon coordination. LAG_TABLE and LAG_MEMBER_TABLE entries in STATE_DB reflect current operational status including LACP port states.
dependencies.name: STATE_DB (Redis DB 6)

dependencies.description: Spanning Tree Protocol runs on the same physical ports as LACP. STP can block a port before LACP has a chance to aggregate it. In SONiC, STP is managed separately but the two protocols interact -- a port must be STP-forwarding for LACPDUs to be exchanged successfully.
dependencies.name: STP (optional, interacts)

dependencies.description: Multi-Chassis LAG uses LACP PortChannels as the client-facing aggregation. MCLAG requires both peers to present the same LACP System ID (same system_mac and system_priority) so the client sees a single LACP endpoint. iccpd (ICCP daemon) synchronizes LAG state between MCLAG peers.
dependencies.name: MCLAG (optional, interacts)

Asymmetric LACP rate configuration
Control-plane CPU saturation causing LACPDU processing delays
STP interaction causing port-channel member to be blocked

Asymmetric port-channel — different number of active members on each side

failure_modes.description: teamd is sending LACPDUs but receiving none from the partner. The actor-timeout fires and the port transitions through EXPIRED to DEFAULTED. The PortChannel may go down or remain up with fewer active members depending on the min-links configuration. Root causes range from physical layer problems to CoPP rate-limiting dropping slow protocol frames on one or both sides.

resolutions.action: Verify physical link: 'show interfaces <member-port> status' -- link must be up
Capture LACPDUs on member port: 'sudo tcpdump -i <member-port> ether proto 0x8809 -c 10 -e'
Check CoPP on partner: verify slow protocol rate limit is not too aggressive
Verify partner LACP config: confirm partner has LACP enabled on the same physical port
Check teamd logs: 'docker exec teamd supervisorctl status' and 'journalctl -u teamd -n 50'
Check syslog for port flap events: 'sudo grep -i lacp /var/log/syslog | tail -30'

root_causes.cause: Physical link issue -- fiber cut, bad SFP, or link down on one side; LACPDU frames are lost
CoPP (Control Plane Policing) rate-limiting slow protocols on the partner switch -- LACPDUs are dropped before reaching the partner CPU
Partner switch not configured for LACP -- port may be in access/trunk mode with no LACP
Partner LACP process crashed or paused -- no LACPDUs being generated on partner side
Spanning tree blocking the port -- STP blocks the physical port before LACP can run
Incorrect VLAN or native VLAN configuration stripping the slow protocol frames
Hardware rate-limiter on the local switch dropping inbound slow protocol (EtherType 0x8809) frames

failure_modes.scenario: LACPDU not received -- port stuck in DEFAULTED or EXPIRED
symptoms: 'teamdctl PortChannel<id> state' shows runner.port.<member>.state as 'defaulted' or 'expired'
'show interfaces PortChannel<id>' shows reduced or zero member ports in Distributing state
PortChannel interface goes down if active members drop below min_links threshold
Periodic log messages from teamd: 'port <member> is going to be expired' in syslog
tcpdump on member port shows outbound LACPDUs but no inbound LACPDUs from partner

verify_commands.confirms_if: State shows 'defaulted' or 'expired' for one or more member ports
verify_commands.look_for: runner.port.<member>.state field -- 'defaulted' or 'expired' confirms no LACPDUs received

verify_commands.confirms_if: Outbound LACPDUs seen but no inbound frames confirms one-way LACPDU loss from partner
verify_commands.look_for: Inbound LACPDU frames from partner MAC -- absence of inbound frames confirms partner is not sending

verify_commands.confirms_if: Link down confirms physical layer failure preventing LACPDU exchange
verify_commands.look_for: Link State field -- must be 'up' for LACPDUs to be exchanged

verify_commands.confirms_if: Repeated expiry log entries confirm ongoing LACPDU loss pattern
verify_commands.look_for: Port expiry or default messages from teamd with timestamps

verify_commands.confirms_if: status=disabled or absent entry confirms port is not in active forwarding state
verify_commands.look_for: status field in STATE_DB for the member port

failure_modes.description: teamd enforces that all member ports in a PortChannel must have identical speed, duplex, and MTU. If a port is added that does not match the existing members, teamd rejects the mismatched port and it never reaches the DISTRIBUTING state. This is a silent failure -- the PortChannel stays up with the compatible members and the mismatched port is excluded without a loud error.

resolutions.action: Check per-member port speed: 'show interfaces <member-port>' for each member
Verify all members have the same speed in CONFIG_DB: 'redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL "PORT|<member-port>"'
Set consistent speed: 'config interface speed <member-port> <speed>'
Verify MTU consistency: 'show interfaces <member-port>' MTU field for all members
Set consistent MTU: 'config interface mtu <member-port> <mtu>'

root_causes.cause: One member port was manually configured to a different speed (e.g., 10G vs 25G) while others are at their negotiated speed
Auto-negotiation is disabled on one port but enabled on others, causing speed disagreement
MTU was changed on a subset of member ports after the PortChannel was created
Half-duplex auto-negotiation result on one port when others are full-duplex
Breakout cable (e.g., 100G -> 4x25G) changing the effective speed of some members unexpectedly

failure_modes.scenario: Speed, duplex, or MTU mismatch between LAG member ports
symptoms: PortChannel interface is up but has fewer active members than configured
'teamdctl PortChannel<id> state' shows the mismatched port in a non-distributing state
'show interfaces PortChannel<id>' member count is lower than expected
teamd logs show 'port <member> is not compatible with aggregator' or similar message
No LACPDU activity logged for the mismatched port even though the link is physically up

verify_commands.confirms_if: Fewer active members than configured confirms some ports are being rejected by teamd
verify_commands.look_for: Member port list and their individual status -- missing or inactive members indicate compatibility rejection

verify_commands.confirms_if: Speed or MTU mismatch between members confirms the root cause
verify_commands.look_for: Speed and MTU fields for each configured member port -- compare across all members

verify_commands.confirms_if: Different speed or mtu values across members in CONFIG_DB confirms misconfiguration
verify_commands.look_for: speed, mtu fields in CONFIG_DB for the member port

verify_commands.command: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel<id> state
verify_commands.confirms_if: Port showing non-selected or non-aggregatable state confirms compatibility rejection
verify_commands.look_for: Per-port state details including whether port is selected and aggregatable

verify_commands.confirms_if: Log messages citing speed, MTU, or duplex incompatibility confirm the mismatch
verify_commands.look_for: teamd log messages about port incompatibility with the aggregator

failure_modes.description: LACP uses the system priority and system MAC address to form a System ID that identifies each LACP endpoint. If the partner's System ID in the actor's LACPDU does not match what the partner believes its own System ID to be, the Sync bit is never set and the port cannot enter the DISTRIBUTING state. In SONiC, this commonly occurs in MCLAG scenarios where the system MAC must be explicitly configured to match on both MCLAG peers.

resolutions.action: For MCLAG: configure 'system_mac' in CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL table to be identical on both MCLAG peers
Verify system MAC in LACPDU capture: 'sudo tcpdump -i <member-port> ether proto 0x8809 -c 5 -e -v'
Check MCLAG system MAC: 'redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL "MCLAG_DOMAIN|<id>"' for mclag_system_mac field
Verify iccpd status if MCLAG: 'docker exec iccpd supervisorctl status'
Restart teamd after correcting MAC config: 'sudo systemctl restart teamd'

root_causes.cause: MCLAG partner MAC not configured: two MCLAG peers have different system MACs so the partner client sees two different LACP endpoints instead of one
System priority mismatch: different system priorities configured on the two ends cause priority-based system selection to fail
Manual system MAC override set incorrectly -- configured MAC does not match partner expectation
teamd using the physical port MAC instead of the configured system MAC after a restart
ICCPD (MCLAG daemon) has not synced the system MAC to teamd on one of the MCLAG peers

failure_modes.scenario: System ID mismatch -- partner system priority or MAC disagreement preventing aggregation
symptoms: 'teamdctl PortChannel<id> state' shows Synchronization bit not set in partner state
Port remains in COLLECTING state but never transitions to DISTRIBUTING
LACPDU capture shows actor and partner system IDs that do not agree
MCLAG PortChannel shows different active member count on each peer
Partner reports 'system ID mismatch' in its own LACP logs

verify_commands.confirms_if: Disagreement between actor's partner-system-ID and partner's own actor-system-ID confirms system ID mismatch
verify_commands.look_for: Actor System and Partner System fields in LACPDU -- compare actor-reported-partner vs partner-reported-actor

verify_commands.command: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel<id> state
verify_commands.confirms_if: Sync bit not set and system IDs differ confirms system ID mismatch preventing aggregation
verify_commands.look_for: partner.system_id and actor.system_id fields; Sync bit (synchronization) in partner state

verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing or different mclag_system_mac confirms MCLAG system MAC not configured
verify_commands.look_for: mclag_system_mac field -- must be identical on both MCLAG peers

verify_commands.confirms_if: Absent system_mac field confirms SONiC is using the default chassis MAC which may differ from the MCLAG peer
verify_commands.look_for: system_mac field in CONFIG_DB for this PortChannel

failure_modes.description: LACP uses an Aggregation Key to determine which ports can be combined into the same LAG. Ports that share an aggregation key and agree on system IDs can aggregate. In SONiC, teamd derives the aggregation key from the PortChannel configuration. If the partner assigns a different key to the corresponding ports, they will not aggregate -- ports will stay in ATTACHED or WAITING state indefinitely.

resolutions.action: Capture LACPDUs and compare actor.key vs partner.key: 'sudo tcpdump -i <member-port> ether proto 0x8809 -c 5 -e -v'
Verify port assignment on partner: confirm partner has correct ports in the matching LAG
Check CONFIG_DB PortChannel membership: 'redis-cli -n 4 KEYS "PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER*"'
If cabling is correct, verify partner LAG configuration matches SONiC PortChannel numbering
Re-seat the PortChannel membership if ports were moved: remove and re-add members

root_causes.cause: Partner has the physical ports assigned to a different LAG group (different key) than the SONiC side
Physical ports are cabled to the wrong PortChannel on one side -- port intended for LAG1 is connected to LAG2 on partner
Static LACP key override configured differently on both sides
teamd key generation algorithm difference between SONiC and non-SONiC partner
Port was moved from one PortChannel to another without updating partner configuration

failure_modes.scenario: Aggregation key mismatch -- ports with different keys cannot join the same LAG
symptoms: LACPDU exchange is occurring but port never reaches DISTRIBUTING state
'teamdctl PortChannel<id> state' shows partner.key does not match actor.key
Port may show as ATTACHED but never transitions to COLLECTING or DISTRIBUTING
Partner shows the port assigned to a different LAG group than expected
Multiple PortChannels are up but with fewer members than configured

verify_commands.confirms_if: Different Actor Key and Partner Key values in captured LACPDU confirm aggregation key mismatch
verify_commands.look_for: Actor Key and Partner Key fields in LACPDU -- they must match for aggregation

verify_commands.command: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel<id> state
verify_commands.confirms_if: Mismatched key values in teamd state confirm aggregation key disagreement
verify_commands.look_for: actor.key and partner.key fields for each member port

verify_commands.confirms_if: Incorrect port-to-PortChannel mapping confirms misconfigured membership
verify_commands.look_for: List of all PortChannel member assignments in CONFIG_DB

verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing expected members while link is up confirms they are not aggregating due to key mismatch
verify_commands.look_for: Active member ports listed and their forwarding state

failure_modes.description: LACP supports two PDU transmission rates: fast (1 LACPDU/second, 3-second timeout) and slow (1 LACPDU/30 seconds, 90-second timeout). If one side is configured for fast rate and the other for slow rate, the fast-rate side will expire waiting for a slow-rate LACPDU after only 3 seconds. The port will oscillate between DISTRIBUTING and EXPIRED states every 3 seconds, causing intermittent packet loss on that LAG member.

resolutions.action: Check SONiC LACP rate: 'redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL "PORTCHANNEL|PortChannel<id>"' -- look for fast_rate field
Align LACP rate: either set both sides to fast rate or both to slow rate
Change SONiC LACP rate: 'config portchannel member del PortChannel<id> <member>' then 'config portchannel member add PortChannel<id> <member>' after updating CONFIG_DB
Verify partner rate: check partner LACP configuration for the corresponding LAG
If fast rate is required for convergence, ensure partner also supports and is configured for fast rate

root_causes.cause: SONiC side configured with 'fast_rate: true' in CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL but partner is configured for slow rate
Partner switch default is slow rate and SONiC was explicitly set to fast rate without coordinating with partner team
LACP rate changed on one side after the LAG was already up, without changing the partner simultaneously
Network configuration management tool applied fast rate to only one side of the link

failure_modes.scenario: Timeout mode mismatch -- fast vs slow LACP rate causing premature expiry
symptoms: PortChannel member port flaps every 3 seconds (fast-rate side timing out on slow-rate partner)
Intermittent packet drops or retransmissions on traffic using the affected PortChannel
'teamdctl PortChannel<id> state' shows port alternating between 'current' and 'expired'
tcpdump shows frequent LACPDUs from local side but partner responds only every 30 seconds
syslog shows repeated 'port going to expire' messages at 3-second intervals

verify_commands.confirms_if: fast_rate=true on SONiC side while partner sends slow LACPDUs confirms the mismatch
verify_commands.look_for: fast_rate field -- 'true' means fast rate (1s PDU / 3s timeout)

verify_commands.confirms_if: Mismatched intervals between local outbound and partner inbound LACPDUs confirm rate mismatch
verify_commands.look_for: Timestamp intervals between inbound LACPDUs -- 1s intervals = fast rate, 30s intervals = slow rate

verify_commands.confirms_if: 3-second expiry cycle in logs confirms fast-rate timeout firing on slow-rate partner
verify_commands.look_for: Repeated port expiry messages at regular 3-second intervals

verify_commands.command: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel<id> state
verify_commands.confirms_if: Short timeout (fast) set locally but partner Timeout bit showing long rate confirms mismatch
verify_commands.look_for: actor.lacpdu_interval and partner's Timeout bit in state field

failure_modes.description: A port completes LACP negotiation and reaches the COLLECTING state (accepting inbound frames) but never transitions to DISTRIBUTING (enabling outbound frames). This means the local side is receiving from the partner but not transmitting. The PortChannel may appear partially up but asymmetric traffic routing can cause drops. This state occurs when the partner has not acknowledged the local port's Collecting state.

resolutions.action: Verify partner state: confirm partner shows Distributing for its side of the same link
Check ASIC programming: 'redis-cli -n 1 KEYS "ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG_MEMBER*"' to confirm LAG member programming
Check orchagent logs for SAI errors during LAG member state change: 'docker logs swss 2>&1 | grep -iE "lag|collecting|distributing" | tail -30'
If partner has max-bundle limit, verify it is set to accommodate all configured members
Restart LACP on the affected port by removing and re-adding it to the PortChannel
Check STP state on partner for the affected physical port

root_causes.cause: Partner implementation bug: partner does not send updated LACPDU with Collecting bit set after port selection
Partner port stuck in a non-forwarding STP state while LACP negotiation succeeded at Layer 2
Hardware programming failure: teamd set port to Collecting but SAI call to enable Distributing failed
Partner has max-bundle limit set lower than the number of ports being negotiated -- it selects some ports as standby and does not enable Distributing on them
Race condition in LACP state machine on partner side after simultaneous port additions

failure_modes.scenario: LAG member stuck in Collecting state -- not transitioning to Distributing
symptoms: 'teamdctl PortChannel<id> state' shows port in 'collecting' but not 'distributing'
Unidirectional traffic only on the affected port -- inbound works, outbound does not
show interfaces PortChannel<id> may show the port as active but with asymmetric counters
Partner side shows the port as disabled for distribution
syslog shows port entered Collecting state but no Distributing transition logged

verify_commands.command: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel<id> state
verify_commands.confirms_if: Port showing collecting=true and distributing=false confirms stuck in Collecting state
verify_commands.look_for: per-port collecting and distributing flags -- collecting=true but distributing=false confirms stuck state

verify_commands.confirms_if: LAG member present in ASIC_DB with egress_disable=true confirms ASIC-level distribution is blocked
verify_commands.look_for: LAG member entries in ASIC_DB and their egress_disable attribute

verify_commands.confirms_if: SAI errors on LAG member state change confirm hardware programming failure
verify_commands.look_for: SAI errors when setting LAG member state, or warnings about member state transitions

verify_commands.confirms_if: Zero TX counters on a member port with non-zero RX confirms port is collecting but not distributing
verify_commands.look_for: Member port TX/RX counters -- asymmetric counters (RX high, TX zero) on a member port suggest Distributing not active

failure_modes.description: teamd is the SONiC daemon responsible for managing LACP and all PortChannel (team device) operations. If teamd crashes, is OOM-killed, or enters a hung state, all PortChannels on the switch go down simultaneously. This is a high-impact event -- all LAG-based connectivity (including LAG-backed BGP sessions, VLAN trunks, and MCLAG) drops at once. teamd runs in the 'teamd' Docker container.

resolutions.action: Check teamd container status: 'docker ps | grep teamd' and 'docker inspect teamd | grep -i status'
Check teamd logs: 'docker logs teamd 2>&1 | tail -50'
Check for OOM events: 'sudo dmesg | grep -i oom | tail -20'
Restart teamd container: 'sudo systemctl restart teamd' or 'sudo docker restart teamd'
After restart, verify PortChannels recover: 'show interfaces PortChannel*'
Check stale teamd socket files: 'ls /var/run/teamd/' -- stale .sock files may prevent restart

root_causes.cause: teamd OOM killed by Linux kernel due to memory pressure -- teamd exceeds its container memory limit
teamd Python/C process crash due to bug or unexpected configuration state
Redis connection loss: teamd cannot reach CONFIG_DB or STATE_DB and terminates
teammgrd wrote invalid configuration to teamd that caused teamd to assert
SONiC image upgrade left inconsistent teamd state files in /var/run/teamd/
Docker daemon restart killed the teamd container unexpectedly

failure_modes.scenario: teamd process crash or hang -- PortChannel goes down
symptoms: All PortChannel interfaces go down simultaneously
'docker ps | grep teamd' shows teamd container as Exited or restarting
'show interfaces PortChannel*' shows all PortChannels as down
syslog shows teamd daemon died or was killed
BGP sessions that peer over PortChannels drop simultaneously
All LAG member ports revert to individual forwarding or are admin-disabled

verify_commands.confirms_if: Exited or non-zero RestartCount confirms teamd has crashed
verify_commands.look_for: teamd container status -- Exited, Restarting, or Up; RestartCount > 0

verify_commands.confirms_if: Fatal error or exception in teamd logs confirms the crash reason
verify_commands.look_for: Exception tracebacks, fatal errors, segfaults, or Redis connection errors

verify_commands.confirms_if: OOM kill event for teamd confirms memory pressure caused the crash
verify_commands.look_for: OOM kill events targeting teamd or the teamd container cgroup

verify_commands.confirms_if: Simultaneous failure of all PortChannels indicates teamd-level failure rather than individual port issues
verify_commands.look_for: Admin and Oper state -- all PortChannels showing Oper=down simultaneously

verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing LAG_TABLE entries in STATE_DB confirm teamd is not running or not updating state
verify_commands.look_for: LAG entries in STATE_DB -- absent entries confirm teamd is not maintaining LAG state

verify_commands.confirms_if: Stale socket files from previous run may prevent teamd from restarting cleanly
verify_commands.look_for: Stale .sock files from previous teamd instance

failure_modes.description: SONiC PortChannels can have more member ports configured than the hardware LAG max-bundle allows. The excess ports are placed in hot-standby mode -- they send and receive LACPDUs but are not in the DISTRIBUTING state. When an active member fails, a standby port should activate within one LACP timeout period. If the standby port fails to activate, the PortChannel loses bandwidth or goes below min_links and drops.

resolutions.action: Check teamd runner config: 'redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL "PORTCHANNEL|PortChannel<id>"' for runner field
Verify member port priorities: 'docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel<id> state' -- check port_priority
Check orchagent for SAI errors on LAG member updates: 'docker logs swss 2>&1 | grep -iE "lag member|SAI_STATUS" | tail -30'
Verify ASIC LAG member table: 'redis-cli -n 1 KEYS "ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG_MEMBER*"'
Check min_links setting vs available physical ports: 'redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL "PORTCHANNEL|PortChannel<id>"'
If standby not activating, try removing and re-adding the standby port to trigger re-selection

root_causes.cause: teamd runner configuration does not have hot-standby correctly enabled -- missing 'hwaddr_policy' or incorrect runner type
SAI call to promote standby LAG member failed -- orchagent could not update the LAG member list in hardware
Port priority values are equal for all members and teamd tie-breaks incorrectly during failover
min_links is set higher than the number of currently active members after failover but standby activation is delayed by LACP timer
orchagent busy or stalled -- SAI API to add new LAG member is queued but not processed
teamd state machine bug on specific SONiC version where standby selection logic fails after multiple successive failures

failure_modes.scenario: Hot-standby member not activating when active member fails
symptoms: Active member port goes down but PortChannel bandwidth does not recover
'show interfaces PortChannel<id>' shows fewer active members after a failure than expected
Hot-standby port shows LACPDU exchange but is not promoted to DISTRIBUTING state
teamd logs show standby port was not selected to replace failed active port
PortChannel goes below min_links threshold and drops even though physical standbys are available

verify_commands.command: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel<id> state
verify_commands.confirms_if: Standby ports showing selected=false while active members failed confirms standby is not being promoted
verify_commands.look_for: Per-port selected and distributing status; port_priority values for all members

verify_commands.confirms_if: min_links value higher than remaining active ports confirms why PortChannel drops instead of failing over to standby
verify_commands.look_for: min_links field and runner configuration; fast_rate setting

verify_commands.command: docker logs swss 2>&1 | grep -iE 'lag member|SAI_STATUS|orchagent.*lag' | tail -30
verify_commands.confirms_if: SAI errors on LAG member add operation confirm orchagent could not activate the standby port in hardware
verify_commands.look_for: SAI errors when adding or activating LAG member after failover event

verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing or unchanged LAG member count after expected failover confirms ASIC-level standby activation failed
verify_commands.look_for: Number of LAG member entries for the affected PortChannel in ASIC_DB

verify_commands.confirms_if: Reduced member count not recovering after active port failure confirms standby activation failure
verify_commands.look_for: Member count and oper state after the active port failure

messages.description: LACP Data Unit — carries actor/partner state and aggregation parameters
key_fields: Actor System Priority
Actor System ID (MAC)
Actor Key
Actor Port Priority
Actor Port Number
Actor State (Activity, Timeout, Aggregation, Sync, Collecting, Distributing, Defaulted, Expired)
Partner System Priority
Partner System ID
Partner Key
Partner Port Priority
Partner Port Number
Partner State
messages.name: LACPDU
messages.when_sent: Every LACP rate interval (1s or 30s) on LACP-enabled ports

messages.description: Used for redistributing frames during aggregation changes
key_fields: Requester Port
Requester System
messages.name: Marker PDU
messages.when_sent: During port-channel membership changes to ensure frame ordering

messages.description: The primary LACP control message exchanged between peers to negotiate and maintain link aggregation. Carried as an Ethernet slow protocol frame (EtherType 0x8809, subtype 0x01). Both actor and partner information is embedded, allowing each side to see what the other knows about the link state.
key_fields: Actor System Priority: local system priority (lower = higher priority; default 65535)
Actor System ID: local system MAC address used as LACP system identifier
Actor Key: aggregation key -- ports with matching keys can join the same LAG
Actor Port Priority: per-port priority (lower = preferred when exceeding max ports)
Actor Port: physical port number identifier
Actor State: 8-bit field -- Activity (LACP active/passive), Timeout (short/long), Aggregation (individual/aggregatable), Synchronization, Collecting, Distributing, Defaulted, Expired
Partner System Priority: partner's system priority as known by actor
Partner System ID: partner's system MAC address as known by actor
Partner Key: partner's aggregation key as known by actor
Partner Port Priority: partner's port priority as known by actor
Partner Port: partner's port number as known by actor
Partner State: partner's 8-bit state field as known by actor
messages.name: LACPDU (LACP Data Unit)
messages.when_sent: Periodically at PDU interval (1s fast, 30s slow); also triggered immediately on state change or port events

messages.description: Reply to a Marker PDU confirming that all frames transmitted on the specified port before the Marker PDU have been received and processed. Echoes the Requester fields back to the originator.
key_fields: Requester System: echoed from the Marker PDU
Requester Port: echoed from the Marker PDU
Requester Transaction ID: echoed from the Marker PDU
messages.name: Marker Response PDU
messages.when_sent: Immediately upon receipt of a Marker PDU from the partner

purpose: Link Aggregation Control Protocol — negotiates and maintains port-channel/bond membership

states.description: Port not attached to any aggregator
states.name: DETACHED

states.description: Waiting for partner LACPDU before attaching
states.name: WAITING

states.description: Port attached to aggregator but not distributing
states.name: ATTACHED

states.description: Receiving frames from partner (not yet distributing)
states.name: COLLECTING

states.description: Fully active in port-channel, sending and receiving
states.name: DISTRIBUTING

states.description: No LACPDU received from partner; using default values
states.name: DEFAULTED

states.description: Partner info has expired; short timeout in effect
states.name: EXPIRED

timers.description: Interval between LACPDU transmissions in long timeout mode
timers.name: LACP rate (long)
timers.tuning_advice: Use long (default) unless sub-second failover needed

timers.description: Interval between LACPDU transmissions in short timeout mode
timers.name: LACP rate (short)
timers.tuning_advice: Use for faster failure detection; increases control-plane load

timers.description: Time to wait before declaring partner down (long timeout)
timers.name: current_while (long)
timers.on_expiry: Partner info expires; port moves to EXPIRED
timers.tuning_advice: Automatically set based on LACP rate

timers.description: Time to wait before declaring partner down (short timeout)
timers.name: current_while (short)
timers.on_expiry: Partner info expires; port moves to EXPIRED

timers.description: Aggregation wait timer — prevents thrashing during startup
timers.name: wait_while

timers.description: Timeout period after which a port is declared EXPIRED if no LACPDU has been received from the partner. In fast mode (LACP rate fast), the timeout is 3 x short timeout (3s). In slow mode (LACP rate slow), the timeout is 3 x long timeout (90s). Controls how quickly LACP detects a dead partner.
timers.name: LACP Actor Timeout
timers.on_expiry: Port transitions from DISTRIBUTING or COLLECTING to EXPIRED, then to DEFAULTED if not recovered

os_defaults.config_path: CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL|<name>: fast_rate field (true enables fast/short timeout)
os_defaults.default: Slow mode (90s timeout) unless 'fast_rate: true' configured in CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER
os_defaults.frr_path: N/A -- LACP is handled by teamd, not FRR

timers.tuning_advice: Use fast rate (3s timeout) for faster failover in data-center environments. Slow rate (90s) reduces control-plane overhead for stable links. Mismatched rates between peers cause premature timeout on the fast-rate side.

timers.description: Interval at which teamd sends LACPDUs to the partner. Slow rate sends one LACPDU every 30 seconds. Fast rate sends one LACPDU every 1 second. The partner's actor-timeout is always 3x the PDU interval (90s slow, 3s fast).
timers.name: LACP PDU Interval
timers.on_expiry: LACPDU is sent to the partner to maintain the LACP session

os_defaults.frr_path: N/A -- LACP is handled by teamd

timers.tuning_advice: Match PDU interval on both sides. If one side is fast and the other is slow, the fast-rate side will timeout waiting for slow-rate LACPDUs.

transitions.trigger: LACP enabled and link comes up
wait_while timer expires
Partner agrees to aggregate (sync flag received)
Partner is collecting (partner collecting flag set)
Partner stopped collecting
Port removed from port-channel or link failure
Partner disagrees — incompatible parameters
Port configuration changed or removed from channel-group

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Partner may not have LACP enabled (static port-channel on remote)
transitions.trigger: No LACPDU received within timeout
LACPDU received from partner
Partner LACPDU not received within timeout
No LACPDU received during short timeout (3s)
LACPDU received from partner (re-sync)
Port enabled and LACP negotiation starts; teamd sends LACPDU to partner and waits for response

transitions.failure_if_stuck: No LACPDU received from partner -- verify physical link, partner LACP config, and CoPP rate limits
transitions.trigger: Partner LACPDU received with matching aggregation key and compatible system parameters; Current While Timer expires and port is selected
Current While Timer expires with no partner LACPDU received; port falls back to default partner values

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Partner not setting Sync bit -- check for system ID or aggregation key mismatch on partner side
transitions.trigger: Aggregator enabled and partner confirms Synchronization bit set; port begins accepting inbound frames

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Partner not confirming Collecting -- check partner LACP state, possible config mismatch or partner implementation bug
transitions.trigger: Partner confirms Collecting state (partner sets Collecting bit in LACPDU); local port enables outbound frame distribution
Partner signals de-selection (Sync bit cleared in LACPDU) or local configuration change; port stops distributing frames
No LACPDU received within the actor-timeout (3s fast, 90s slow); actor timer fires
LACPDU received from partner before EXPIRED timeout completes; port resynchronizes

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Persistent LACPDU loss -- check physical layer, CoPP, and partner control-plane health
transitions.trigger: No LACPDU received during EXPIRED timer; port fully falls back to default partner values and may be removed from the LAG
LACPDU received from partner; port restarts negotiation

notes.note: LACP available without explicit feature enable. Port-channel interface auto-created with 'channel-group' command. 'lacp rate fast' supported. Supports cross-stack EtherChannel on stackable platforms.

Bundle interfaces used instead of port-channel. Configure as 'interface Bundle-Ether <N>' and 'bundle id <N> mode active' under member interface. LACP rate configurable per bundle. Min-links and max-active supported.

LACP enabled by default with 'feature lacp'. Port-channel created automatically with 'channel-group N mode active'. Supports 'lacp rate fast' for 1s LACPDU interval. Max 32 members per port-channel (16 active, 16 hot-standby).
vPC requires LACP; static port-channels not supported in vPC. LACP system-mac is automatically synchronized between vPC peers.

relevant_fields: min_links: minimum number of active member links for PortChannel to be operationally up
mtu: MTU for the PortChannel logical interface
fast_rate: true/false -- enables fast LACP rate (1s PDU interval, 3s timeout)
lacp_key: optional explicit LACP aggregation key override
tpid: optional TPID for QinQ configurations
mc_lag_id: MCLAG domain ID if this PortChannel is part of an MCLAG
system_mac: optional LACP system MAC override (required for MCLAG)
(key presence indicates membership -- no additional fields required for basic membership)
learn_mode: MAC learning mode on the LAG
status: enabled/disabled -- reflects whether teamd has this port in active distributing state
status: active/inactive LACP member state as maintained by teamsyncd

frr_mapping.config_managed_by: teammgrd (reads CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL and PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER, writes /etc/teamd/*.conf)
frr_mapping.frr_process: N/A -- LACP is not managed by FRR; it is managed by teamd
frr_mapping.teamd_config_dir: /etc/teamd/ (one JSON config file per PortChannel, generated by teammgrd)
frr_mapping.teamd_socket_dir: /var/run/teamd/ (one .sock file per PortChannel for teamdctl communication)

key_commands: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel<id> state

notes.note: In SONiC, LACP is managed by teamd running in the 'teamd' Docker container. teamd uses the Linux kernel's team driver (not the bond driver). Each PortChannel is a team device in the Linux kernel. The teamd process manages LACPDU exchange and aggregator selection. Do NOT use 'ip link' commands to manually add/remove ports from a team device -- use SONiC CLI ('config portchannel member add/del') which writes to CONFIG_DB and lets teammgrd/teamd handle it properly.

The SONiC LACP pipeline is: CONFIG_DB -> teammgrd (writes teamd JSON config) -> teamd (LACP state machine) -> teamsyncd (netlink listener) -> APPL_DB LAG_MEMBER_TABLE -> orchagent -> ASIC_DB -> syncd -> ASIC. A failure at any stage stops LAG state from reaching hardware. When a PortChannel member does not forward traffic, always check both the teamd state and the ASIC_DB LAG member programming.

The minimum-links (min_links) parameter controls when a PortChannel is operationally down. If the number of active (DISTRIBUTING) member ports drops below min_links, the PortChannel interface goes operationally down even if some members are still physically up. The default min_links is 1. Set min_links higher only if you explicitly need the PortChannel to fail when redundancy is lost -- in many deployments min_links=1 is the correct setting.
notes.source_ref: SONiC PortChannel design and sonic-swss/orchagent/portsorch.cpp (LAG handling)

notes.note: For MCLAG deployments, both MCLAG peers must present the same LACP System ID (system_mac + system_priority) to the client switch. The client switch sees a single LACP endpoint spanning both physical switches. Configure 'mclag_system_mac' in CONFIG_DB MCLAG_DOMAIN table and ensure it propagates to the PORTCHANNEL config on both peers via iccpd. Failure to match system MACs is the most common MCLAG LACP failure and causes split-brain LAG behavior.

teamd configuration files are written by teammgrd to /etc/teamd/<PortChannelName>.conf as JSON. These files are the live configuration teamd reads. Do not manually edit these files -- they are regenerated by teammgrd from CONFIG_DB on every teamd restart or configuration change. Manual edits will be overwritten. This is the same CONFIG_DB-as-source-of-truth pattern used by bgpcfgd for BGP.

LACPDUs use Ethernet EtherType 0x8809 (IEEE Slow Protocols). These frames are sent to the well-known multicast MAC 01:80:C2:00:00:02. In SONiC, CoPP (Control Plane Policing) rate-limits these frames as they go to the CPU. The default CoPP rate for slow protocols is typically 1000 pps, which is more than sufficient for LACP. However, custom CoPP configurations that are too aggressive can drop LACPDUs and cause LACP timeouts. Check CoPP config if LACPDU loss is suspected but physical layer is healthy.

SONiC 202211 and later versions support PortChannel per-member port hashing for load balancing. The hash is computed over packet fields (IP src/dst, TCP/UDP ports, etc.) by orchagent and programmed into the ASIC. If traffic appears unbalanced across LAG members (one member at 100%, others idle), check the load-balance hash configuration: 'show hash'. Asymmetric traffic patterns (e.g., all traffic has the same source IP) cannot be fixed by LACP -- this is a hash policy issue, not an LACP issue.
notes.source_ref: SONiC load balance design and sonic-swss/orchagent/portsorch.cpp (LAG hash handling)

notes.note: teamd in SONiC supports both 'activebackup' and 'lacp' runner types. The LACP runner is used for dynamic LAG negotiation. The 'activebackup' runner does NOT use LACP -- it is a simple active/standby without negotiation. Verify the runner type in the teamd config file or via 'teamdctl <PortChannel> config dump' to confirm LACP is actually running and not a static bond.
